mission.title: envoy: Add support for Connect RPC protocol
mission.description: Add support for Connect RPC protocol. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
*Title*: *Add support for the Connect RPC protocol, on par with gRPC and gRPC-Web*

*Description*:
Add two features to support the [Connect RPC protocol](https://connect.build/docs/protocol):

1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add support for Connect RPC protocol
steps.description:
Implements two Connect filters:
- `connect_stats`: Similar to `grpc_stats`. Counts messages and success/failure as stats.
- `connect_grpc_bridge`: Provides a bridge from Connect clients to gRPC servers.

The [Buf Connect](https://connect.build/) protocol provides similar features to gRPC, but supports simple HTTP/1.1 requests for unary procedure calls and does not require trailers for streaming procedure calls.

The stats filter is similar to `grpc_stats`, as mentioned prior. It could

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add support for Connect RPC protocol" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Implements two Connect filters:
- `connect_stats`: Similar to `grpc_stats`. Counts messages and success/failure as stats.
- `connect_grpc_bridge`: Provides a bridge from Connect clients to gRPC servers.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
